summary: PR 2616 split Kirby attack-dash code out of ftkirby.c/ftkirbyspecialn.c into a new ftkirbyattackdash.c/.h translation unit, linked it as Matching in configure.py, and adjusted GALE01 split ranges plus includes that referenced the moved declarations.

key_files.role: New split source file containing ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68 and Kirby attack-dash/attack-dash-air callbacks formerly in adjacent Kirby files.
New public header for exported ftkirbyattackdash functions; internal callback helpers are not exported.
Removed attack-dash functions from special-N file and retained special-N-local callback inline; added default breaks in two switch statements.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyspecialn.c added ftKirby/inlines.h and default break cases

key_files.role: Removed ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68 implementation and added include of the new attackdash header.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirby.c removes ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68 and float externs

key_files.role: Adjusted text/sdata2 ownership boundaries and inserted ftkirbyattackdash split range.
Links the new ftkirbyattackdash object as Matching.
Updates include dependency from broad ftkirby.h to ftkirbyattackdash.h.
Updates include dependency to ftkirbyattackdash.h and adjusts item include set.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:itkirbycutterbeam.c include replacement and itgroundcoll include

what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c with attack-dash entry/callback functions spanning addresses 0F1F68 through 0F21C0.
Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.h with declarations for exported attack-dash functions while leaving fn_800F1FDC and fn_800F20C4 static inside the new C file.
Moved attack-dash functions out of ftkirbyspecialn.c and moved ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68 out of ftkirby.c.
Changed GALE01 split ranges so ftkirby.c ends at 0x800F1F68, ftkirbyattackdash.c owns 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8, and ftkirbyspecialn.c starts at 0x800F21E8.
Added ftkirbyattackdash.c to configure.py as Object(Matching, ...).
Updated dependent includes in ftCo_AttackDash.c, ftkirby.c, and itkirbycutterbeam.c to reference the new focused header.
Replaced ftkirby.c extern float symbols used in ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68 with inline float literals 0.0f and 1.0f in the moved function.
Added default: break cases to two switch statements in ftKb_CaSpecialN_Phys and ftKb_CaSpecialAirN_Phys.

smart_moves.text: The split kept the new object narrowly scoped by moving only the 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8 attack-dash range and updating split boundaries on both neighboring Kirby translation units.
Public declarations were moved from the broad ftkirby.h header into ftkirbyattackdash.h, while local transition callbacks fn_800F1FDC and fn_800F20C4 became static declarations in the new C file.
Dependent files were changed to include ftkirbyattackdash.h instead of ftkirby.h where the moved attack-dash API was the apparent dependency.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a newly matched fighter subrange, update all three surfaces together: split ownership in config/GALE01/splits.txt, object classification in configure.py, and the owning header for moved declarations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyattackdash.h added and ftkirby.h declarations removed

decomp_lessons.lesson: A split can reduce header coupling by moving only the declarations needed by outside users into the new focused header and updating includes to that header.
For moved code, helper functions that are only used inside the new translation unit can be made static instead of remaining exported through the old aggregate header.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyattackdash.c declares static fn_800F1FDC and fn_800F20C4
raw_diff:ftkirby.h removes fn_800F1FDC and fn_800F20C4 declarations

naming_conventions.observation: The new file and header preserve existing Kirby attack-dash function names with address comments for unresolved or partially semantic functions.
Address-style names were kept for helpers without proven semantics and scoped static in the new file.
The split file name follows the lowercase ftKirby file naming style and matches the PR title's target component.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Link the split file as Matching after assigning it an exact text and sdata2 range.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:splits.txt ftkirbyattackdash.c text and sdata2 ranges
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR evidence shows Matching classification but does not include objdiff or build logs.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use literals 0.0f and 1.0f in the moved ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68 instead of carrying over address-named float externs from ftkirby.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This aligns with avoiding unnecessary named literal storage, but the slice does not provide explicit author explanation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add default: break cases in two switch statements near the remaining special-N code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftkirbyspecialn.c adds default break in ftKb_CaSpecialN_Phys
raw_diff:ftkirbyspecialn.c adds default break in ftKb_CaSpecialAirN_Phys
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Likely codegen or warning/include fallout, but no comment or verifier evidence explains the purpose.

evidence_refs: metadata_json:0 review comments, 0 issue comments, 0 reviews
notes: Changed files and code movements are strongly evidenced by the full raw diff.
The PR body is empty and there are no review or issue comments, so reviewer intent and author rationale are not evidenced.
configure.py labels the new object Matching, but the slice does not include build, objdiff, or regression output; do not promote a verified-match claim beyond the configure classification.
Some edits, such as added default breaks and include-set adjustments, are visible but not explained by comments.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split/link changes need synchronized split, configure, and header updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2616 shows a focused fighter subrange split where config/GALE01/splits.txt, configure.py Matching object registration, and public header ownership were updated together for ftkirbyattackdash.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep internal moved callbacks static after a TU split
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2616 moved attack-dash callbacks into ftkirbyattackdash.c and left fn_800F1FDC/fn_800F20C4 as file-local static helpers instead of exporting them from ftkirby.h.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyattackdash split file ownership
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyattackdash.c was introduced as a Kirby attack-dash split owning text 0x800F1F68-0x800F21E8 and sdata2 0x804D9368-0x804D9370 in config/GALE01/splits.txt, and was added to configure.py as Object(Matching, ...).
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path/split fact for future Kirby attack-dash lookups; curator should verify against current source before promoting.

source_update_candidates.title: Focused headers for split translation units
source_update_candidates.text: When splitting a translation unit, consider moving the exported declarations into a focused header and updating dependent includes to avoid retaining broad aggregate header dependencies.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is supported as a candidate pattern by the PR diff, but should be curated against broader project header practice before becoming a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not claim objdiff/build verification from this slice; only configure.py's Matching classification is visible.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; there are no review comments or reviews in the loaded metadata.
Do not generalize the added default: break cases into a standard without additional matching evidence.
